summary: Reviewed the live-event reliability changes for architectural fit, data-flow consistency, and boundary contracts between admin actions, realtime clients, shared helpers, and migrations. The main risks are duplicated live-state derivations that can drift from the accepted `state_version` snapshot, a broken client/server form contract for locked games, and connection health modeling that does not match the stated Realtime-plus-polling ownership model.
appears_sound: `src/proxy.ts:6` keeps the auth proxy matcher narrowly scoped to `/admin/:path*`, `/host/:path*`, and `/login`, preserving the public-route performance boundary.

`supabase/migrations/20260430120000_add_state_version.sql:14` centralizes version increments in a database trigger, and the sync function copies `state_version` into `game_states_public`, which fits the server-as-source-of-truth model.

`src/app/display/[sessionId]/page.tsx:14` and `src/app/player/[sessionId]/page.tsx:14` use narrow public select lists against `game_states_public`, avoiding leakage of private `game_states.number_sequence` to public clients.
`src/lib/prize-validation.ts:21` is a pure shared helper reused by client and server validation without importing server-only dependencies.

findings.description: Locked-game editing breaks the client/server action contract: the UI disables the structural fieldset, but disabled controls are omitted from `FormData`. `updateGame` then sees missing `type`, `stages`, `snowball_pot_id`, and prize fields as attempted structural/prize changes, so even allowed edits like name, order, colour, or notes are rejected.
findings.evidence: The locked structural fields are rendered inside `<fieldset disabled={isGameLocked}>` in `session-detail.tsx:543`, while `updateGame` reads those same fields from `formData` before comparing locked values in `src/app/admin/sessions/[id]/actions.ts:160`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If the intended product rule is that started games cannot be edited at all, the server rejection is acceptable, but the enabled non-structural inputs and modal copy say only prize/type/pot/stage configuration is locked.

findings.description: The `state_version` freshness gate is applied only to `currentGameState`, while dependent display state is still updated from the incoming payload. A stale realtime payload can be rejected for numbers/status but still overwrite `currentPrizeText`, leaving the display/player prize out of sync with the accepted game-state snapshot.
findings.evidence: `display-ui.tsx:176` freshness-gates `setCurrentGameState`, then unconditionally derives `currentPrizeText` from `incoming.current_stage_index`; `player-ui.tsx:166` uses the same pattern.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If stale payloads always repeat the same stage, no visible drift occurs, but stale stage-change payloads are exactly the case `state_version` was added to prevent.

findings.description: The realtime subscription listens to `event: '*'` but treats every payload as an upsert with a valid `payload.new` row. Delete events from the public mirror can leave stale state on display/player, or at minimum route an invalid snapshot through the freshness and prize-derivation path.
findings.evidence: `display-ui.tsx:170` subscribes to all events and `display-ui.tsx:173` casts `payload.new as GameState`; the migration sync function deletes from `game_states_public` on source deletes in `supabase/migrations/20260430120000_add_state_version.sql:35`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The truncated polling/session-update paths may clear state shortly after a reset, but the realtime handler itself does not honor the delete branch of its subscribed contract.
findings.what_would_confirm: Test an open display/player during `resetSession` or a `game_states` delete and verify whether stale called numbers remain until polling or refresh.

findings.description: Connection health collapses browser, polling, and realtime into a single `healthy` flag, which does not match the stated model that the banner appears when Realtime and polling both stall. A single poll failure can mark the page unhealthy while realtime is still delivering data, and a `SUBSCRIBED` status can mark it healthy without proving fresh game-state updates are arriving.
findings.evidence: `reduceHealth` flips unhealthy on any `poll-failure` at `connection-health.ts:37` and flips healthy on realtime `SUBSCRIBED` at `connection-health.ts:43`, rather than tracking independent transport freshness and combining them in the selector.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If the intended policy is to warn on any transport problem rather than both transports stalling, this is a spec mismatch instead of an implementation defect.
findings.what_would_confirm: Run reducer-level scenarios for realtime-subscribed plus poll-failing, and poll-success plus realtime-closed, against the intended banner/refresh policy.

empty_categories: No proxy matcher broadening found; public display/player routes still bypass middleware.
No unique constraint was added to `winners` in the provided migrations, so multi-winner support is not blocked at the schema level.
No public client read of private `game_states` was found in the visible display/player code; public surfaces use `game_states_public`.
No server-only dependency leak was found in the shared prize-validation helper.
